title: Light Herbal Ukrainian Fish Pilaf
description: A refreshing and energizing Ukrainian dish featuring fish seasoning and herbal tadka, suitable for an ayurvedic tamasic diet. This pilaf is pillowy and flavorful, making it a great option for a light meal.
instructions: Rinse the brown rice in a fine mesh strainer and drain well.
In a large saucepan, heat the olive oil over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the grated carrots and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
Add the fish seasoning, brown rice, and water to the saucepan, stirring well to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and cover the saucepan with a tight-fitting lid.
Simmer the pilaf for 20 minutes, or until the liquid has been absorbed and the rice is tender.
Stir in the chopped dill and parsley, and season with lemon juice to taste.
Serve the pilaf hot, garnished with additional herbs if desired.
